prompt: Implement `twoSum(nums: number[], target: number): [number, number]` returning the indices of the two numbers that add to target. Exactly one solution exists; do not reuse an index.
canonicalSolution: export function twoSum(nums: number[], target: number): [number, number] { const seen = new Map<number, number>(); for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { const need = target - nums[i]; if (seen.has(need)) return [seen.get(need)!, i]; seen.set(nums[i], i); } throw new Error('no solution'); }
test: assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([2,7,11,15],9))==='[0,1]'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([3,2,4],6))==='[1,2]'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([3,3],6))==='[0,1]'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([-1,-2,-3,-4,-5],-8))==='[2,4]', 'negative numbers'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([0,4,3,0],0))==='[0,3]', 'zeros and reuse guard'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([3,100,200,300,400,500,600,11],14))==='[0,7]', 'summands far apart'); assert(JSON.stringify(twoSum([100,200,300,400,500],900))==='[3,4]', 'large values far apart');
